About the award

A named, merit-based scholarship from UVA's School of Data Science, supported by the University's Bicentennial Scholars Fund.

It recognizes an incoming student of exceptional academic ability, leadership, and promise in the field. Khalid is the inaugural recipient — the first-ever Data Science Bicentennial (Stephenson) Scholar.

3×
Dean's List semesters
↻ flip
Dean's List at UVA

A semester honor awarded to students who earn a high GPA while carrying a full graded course load (15+ credits).

It recognizes sustained academic excellence. Khalid has earned it across 3 semesters.

Selected work

Projects

Self-directed, end-to-end builds — from raw data to a result someone can act on.

Econometrics · ML · Collaboration

Air Fryer Market — Demand & Pricing

A two-person collaboration building an end-to-end demand estimation pipeline on a 5-year, 10-brand dataset (2019–2023), translating an econometric model into pricing strategy.

0.76
Model R²
10
Brands
~31%
Share drop / +$10
  • Estimated a multinomial logit demand model with brand & year fixed effects
  • Computed unit costs, markups, and profit indices via the Lerner condition — flagged Ninja as top performer
  • Delivered a 6-slide brief for a non-technical audience

Predictive analytics

Soccer Player Market Valuation

Analysis of 18,000+ FIFA player records to surface the key drivers of individual market value across age, position, potential, and nationality.

18k+
Player records
Leak-safe
Feature design
  • Log transforms + position-specific segmentation to reveal age–performance dynamics
  • Fully reproducible clean → filter → validate → transform pipeline
  • Designed an executive-ready infographic of the findings

Click infographic to enlarge
Deep learning

Stellar Object Classification

A neural network that classifies astrophysical objects from photometric data, tuned and validated against a probabilistic baseline. Completed on UVA's Rivanna high-performance computing cluster per the professor's instructions.

97%
Accuracy
0.99
AUC-ROC
9,990
Observations
  • Optimized an MLPClassifier with GridSearchCV hyperparameter tuning
  • Engineered a reduced photometric feature set to prevent data leakage
  • Benchmarked against Gaussian Naïve Bayes; validated with confusion matrices
scikit-learnSciPyNumPyRivanna HPC

Relevant Experience

Roles where I put data science to work directly.

May 2025 – Jul 2025 · Remote

Data Analysis Intern

Riipen Micro-Internship · Remote
  • Designed a Python-based, user-facing data dashboard on synthetic structured datasets to assess work-life blind spots and promote equity-focused analytics for stakeholder review
  • Collaborated with a 4-person distributed team to evaluate AI chatbot performance through a proprietary testing framework, producing structured written documentation and reports of the findings

  • Completed the Google Data Analytics Professional Certificate, covering core SQL, data cleaning, and end-to-end analytical workflows
  • Delivered written reports and technical documentation tailored to non-technical stakeholders, sharpening clear analytical communication in a professional, asynchronous team environment

A bit more

About

I'm a Data Science student at the University of Virginia, with a double concentration in Analytics (AI) and Systems (ML) and a minor in General Business. The mix is intentional — I like problems where the model and the business decision both matter.

My projects are self-directed and built end-to-end: clean the raw data, build something that works, and translate the result into a recommendation a non-technical person can act on. Whether it's pricing strategy from a demand model or a neural net classifying stars, I care about the full path from data to decision.

Outside of class I run my own home-call barbershop — 100+ clients and a six-figure-view social presence built from scratch. It taught me as much about marketing, scheduling, and client relationships as any course has.

Education

  • BS Data Science — University of Virginia
  • Concentrations: Analytics (AI) & Systems (ML)
  • Minor: General Business
  • GPA: 3.80 · Class of 2028

Academic Interests

  • Machine learning & applied AI
  • Robotics & AI
  • Sports & market analytics
  • Entrepreneurship
